summary: Joe Reis argues that December, not January 1st, is the optimal time to start working on goals because waiting until New Year leads to abandoned resolutions by February. He advocates confronting the 'scary goals' that terrify you most, as these are likely the ones that will actually change your life.
key_points: Start goals in December so that by January, the habit is already established
Focus on process and daily habits rather than obsessing over outcomes
Protect your time by saying no to low-value obligations
Use AI tools as strategic partners to break down intimidating goals into actionable steps
The goals that scare you most are usually the ones worth pursuing
money_quotes: Usually, the work that will actually change your life is the work you are most scared to do.
Most people wait until January 1st to set goals. By February or March, those goals are dead.
I usually hate that word because people obsess over the outcome instead of the process.
I'm notorious for saying 'no,' and very rarely have I regretted it.
Don't let another year slip by.
key_insight: The goals that terrify you and constantly nag at the back of your mind are the ones most worth pursuing—and December is the time to confront them before another year slips away.
